text: The surgeon applies an omental patch to the appendiceal stump, using a minimally invasive robotic approach.
Three trocars are placed in a vertical line along the left rectus abdominis muscle, with the center incision at the level of the umbilicus, and additional incisions above and below.
The surgeon applies an omental gram patch over the appendiceal stump to reinforce the closure, avoiding a larger resection such as ileocecectomy.

The surgeon places interrupted vicryl sutures over the purse string suture to reinforce the closure of the appendiceal stump. Two O-silk sutures are positioned at the superior and inferior base of the stump, and tying these sutures over the omental patch provides a solid approximation of the omentum to the inflamed stump.
